title: Radiologists
description: Diagnose and treat diseases and injuries using medical imaging techniques, such as x rays, magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), nuclear medicine, and ultrasounds. May perform minimally invasive medical procedures and tests.

tasks.text: Prepare comprehensive interpretive reports of findings.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform or interpret the outcomes of diagnostic imaging procedures including magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), computer tomography (CT), positron emission tomography (PET), nuclear cardiology treadmill studies, mammography, or ultrasound.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Document the performance, interpretation, or outcomes of all procedures performed.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Communicate examination results or diagnostic information to referring physicians, patients, or families.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Obtain patients' histories from electronic records, patient interviews, dictated reports, or by communicating with referring clinicians.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Review or transmit images and information using picture archiving or communications systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Confer with medical professionals regarding image-based diagnoses.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Recognize or treat complications during and after procedures, including blood pressure problems, pain, oversedation, or bleeding.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop or monitor procedures to ensure adequate quality control of images.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide counseling to radiologic patients to explain the processes, risks, benefits, or alternative treatments.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Establish or enforce standards for protection of patients or personnel.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Coordinate radiological services with other medical activities.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Instruct radiologic staff in desired techniques, positions, or projections.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Participate in continuing education activities to maintain and develop expertise.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Participate in quality improvement activities including discussions of areas where risk of error is high.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform interventional procedures such as image-guided biopsy, percutaneous transluminal angioplasty, transhepatic biliary drainage, or nephrostomy catheter placement.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop treatment plans for radiology patients.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Administer radioisotopes to clinical patients or research subjects.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Advise other physicians of the clinical indications, limitations, assessments, or risks of diagnostic and therapeutic applications of radioactive materials.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Calculate, measure, or prepare radioisotope dosages.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Check and approve the quality of diagnostic images before patients are discharged.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Compare nuclear medicine procedures with other types of procedures, such as computed tomography, ultrasonography, nuclear magnetic resonance imaging, and angiography.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Direct nuclear medicine technologists or technicians regarding desired dosages, techniques, positions, and projections.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Establish and enforce radiation protection standards for patients and staff.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Formulate plans and procedures for nuclear medicine departments.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Monitor handling of radioactive materials to ensure that established procedures are followed.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prescribe radionuclides and dosages to be administered to individual patients.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Review procedure requests and patients' medical histories to determine applicability of procedures and radioisotopes to be used.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Teach nuclear medicine, diagnostic radiology, or other specialties at graduate educational level.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Test dosage evaluation instruments and survey meters to ensure they are operating properly.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
